name: KrillApp.Executor.OutgoingWebHook
title: Outgoing WebHook
description: Sends an HTTP request to an external URL when invoked. Supports GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, and DELETE methods. For POST/PUT/PATCH, the input node is serialized as JSON for the request body. Custom headers and query parameters can be configured. The HTTP response body is stored in the webhook node's own meta.snapshot; to persist it, wire a DataPoint to observe the webhook (sources and inputs).
shortDescription: Sends an HTTP request to an external URL and stores the response in its own snapshot.
llmPurpose: Sends an HTTP request to a configured URL with optional body, headers, and parameters, and stores the response in its own meta.snapshot for observers to pull.
llmBehavior: invoked when an observed source completes (SOURCE_INVOKED)
constructs HTTP request with URL, method, headers, query params
serializes input node as JSON body for POST/PUT/PATCH
sends HTTP request to external endpoint
stores response body in its own meta.snapshot; nodes observing the webhook are invoked
llmOutputs: HTTP response body stored in own meta.snapshot; observers pull it from there
llmTypicalUseCases: send sensor data to an external API
notify a Slack or Teams channel
post data to a cloud platform
llmPromptHints: send this data to an API
llmCreationHints: Create an OutgoingWebHook when the user wants to send data to an external HTTP endpoint. Requires a URL and method. List the invoking trigger in sources with SOURCE_INVOKED; optionally list a node in inputs to serialize as the request body, plus headers and query params. To persist the response body, wire a DataPoint to observe the webhook via its own sources+inputs.

llmConnectionHints.role: action node that sends HTTP requests and stores the response in its own snapshot
llmConnectionHints.wiring: Observer-side: list the trigger in sources with SOURCE_INVOKED; list the node to serialize as the request body in inputs; a DataPoint wanting the response body observes this webhook via its own sources+inputs.

llmExamples: Send this sensor reading to my cloud API.
POST a notification to Slack when the temperature is high.
Call this REST endpoint with the latest data.
Send a GET request to check a remote service status.
